criteria.description: Output path — entry appended to docs/SPEC.md only
criteria.check: spec-keeper writes only to docs/SPEC.md. Any Write or Edit call targeting sessions/ or any path other than docs/SPEC.md is a FAIL of this criterion.

criteria.description: Verbatim content — all section text copied from spec_path, nothing invented
criteria.check: Every field in the appended section (Problem, Goals, Out of scope, Acceptance criteria) is copied verbatim from the spec at spec_path (the repo-root SPEC.md). Content that does not appear in the spec is a FAIL of this criterion.

criteria.description: Additive-only and idempotent — existing content is not modified, no duplicate entries
criteria.check: spec-keeper only appends to docs/SPEC.md. Any Edit that modifies pre-existing content (not just adding at the end) is a FAIL. If a section heading for the same date+slug already exists, spec-keeper must no-op rather than append a duplicate; appending a duplicate entry is a FAIL.

criteria.description: Date derived from run_id — not from system clock or hardcoded
criteria.check: The YYYY-MM-DD date in the appended section is derived from the first 8 characters of run_id, reformatted as YYYY-MM-DD. A date that does not match this derivation is a FAIL of this criterion.
